prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of benzaldehyde (14 atoms: O, C×7, H×6) computed at the DFT level using B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard', PySCF RKS backend), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Mulliken partial charges were used for the finite-difference Fukui condensed indices (N, N+1, N−1 calculations). The headline value reported is the maximum f+ (electrophilic Fukui index) = 0.39938968, located at atom 3 (C, ring carbon). The most nucleophilic site (largest f−) is atom 1 (O, carbonyl oxygen) with f− = 0.23839906. Integrity status: ok, trustworthy: true. Fukui charge conservation checks passed: Σf+ = 1.0000, Σf− = 1.0000. No engine warnings were reported. CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --charge 0 --mult 1 --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard benzaldehyde.xyz. Plot written to: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260706-173625_benzaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png. Note: negative Fukui values on hydrogen atoms are a known Mulliken artifact at large basis sets.
